sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: 엔브리지, 2026년 1분기 EBITDA 58억 달러로 전년 대비 안정적, DCF 39억 달러로 증가, 확보 백로그 400억 달러로 확대 및 2026년 가이던스 재확인

keyPoints.text: 2026년 1분기 조정 EBITDA는 58억 달러로 전년과 비슷한 수준을 유지했습니다.
분배가능현금흐름(DCF)은 39억 달러로 전년의 38억 달러 대비 개선됐습니다.
확보된 프로젝트 백로그가 400억 달러로 성장하며 중장기 성장 가시성이 높아졌습니다.

riskChanges.text: 영업활동 현금흐름이 23억 달러로 전년의 31억 달러 대비 감소해 단기 현금 창출 압박이 존재합니다.

businessChanges.text: 캐나다 온타리오주 던 허브에서 비규제 천연가스 저장용량 확장 및 플래나건 사우스·서던 액세스 파이프라인 공개 모집을 발표하며 메인라인 최적화 2단계를 지원합니다.
Cone 프로젝트(7억 달러 규모)를 승인하며 미국 천연가스 수요 대응을 위한 신규 프로젝트를 추가했습니다.
경영진은 다각화된 저위험 사업 모델의 일관된 성과를 강조하며 2026년 연간 가이던스를 재확인했습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 1분기 조정 EBITDA는 58억 달러로 전년 동기와 동등한 수준을 유지했습니다.
분배가능현금흐름(DCF)은 39억 달러로 전년 38억 달러 대비 증가했습니다.
주당 0.98달러 배당금을 선언하며 안정적인 주주환원을 이어갔습니다.
확보 백로그 400억 달러는 장기 파이프라인 프로젝트 기반 성장의 가시성을 뒷받침합니다.

watchItems.text: 영업현금흐름 감소(23억 달러 vs 전년 31억 달러)가 일시적 요인인지 구조적 변화인지 확인이 필요합니다.
400억 달러 백로그의 실행 속도와 메인라인 최적화 2단계 장기계약 확보 여부가 향후 성장의 핵심입니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: EX-99.1 2 enb-ex99_1.htm EX-99.1 EX-99.1 NEWS RELEASE Enbridge Reports Strong First Quarter Results, Reaffirms 2026 Financial Guidance, and Grows Secured Backlog to $40 Billion CALGARY, AB, May 8, 2026 /CNW/ - Enbridge Inc. (Enbridge or the Company) (TSX:ENB) (NYSE:ENB) today reported first quarter 2026 financial results, reaffirmed its 2026 financial guidance and provided a quarterly business update. Highlights (All financial figures are unaudited and in Canadian dollars unless otherwise noted. * identifies non-GAAP financial measures. Please refer to Non-GAAP Reconciliations Appendices. ) • First quarter GAAP earnings attributable to common shareholders of $1.7 billion or $0.77 per common share, compared with GAAP earnings attributable to common shareholders of $2.  Normalized numeric aliases: $40 Billion = 400억 달러; $1.7 billion = 17억 달러; $2.3 billion = 23억 달러; $2.1 billion = 21억 달러; $40 = 40달러; $1.7 = 1.7달러; $0.77 = 0.77달러; $2.3 = 2.3달러; $2.1 = 2.1달러; $1.04 = 1.04달러; $0.98 = 0.98달러.

$2.2 billion or $1.03 per common share in 2025 • Adjusted earnings before interest, income taxes and depreciation and amortization (EBITDA)* of $5.8 billion, in-line with 2025 • Cash provided by operating activities of $2.3 billion, compared with $3.1 billion in 2025 • Distributable cash flow (DCF)* of $3.9 billion, compared with $3.8 billion in 2025 • Reaffirmed 2026 full year financial guidance and multi-year financial outlook • Sanctioned US$0.7 billion Cone project, a 300 MW onshore wind facility in Texas supporting Meta Platforms, Inc. (Meta)'s data center operations under a long-term power purchase agreement • Sanctioned US$0.4 billion Tres Palacios expansion, adding 25 Bcf of  Normalized numeric aliases: $2.2 billion = 22억 달러; $5.8 billion = 58억 달러; $2.3 billion = 23억 달러; $3.1 billion = 31억 달러; $3.9 billion = 39억 달러; $3.8 billion = 38억 달러; $0.7 billion = 7억 달러; $0.4 billion = 4억 달러; $2.2 = 2.2달러; $3.9 = 3.9달러; $3.1 = 3.1달러; $3.8 = 3.8달러; $0.7 = 0.7달러; $0.4 = 0.4달러; $0.1 = 0.1달러.

m under long-term contracts • Announced an 8 Bcf expansion of unregulated natural gas storage at the Dawn Hub in Ontario • Announced binding open seasons on the Flanagan South Pipeline (FSP) and Southern Access Extension Pipeline (SAX), supporting Mainline Optimization Phase 2 • Completed successful open season on the Spearhead Pipeline, substantially extending commitments beyond 2030 1 CEO COMMENT Greg Ebel, President and CEO commented the following: "The past several months have presented some of the most volatile and complex conditions the global energy sector has faced in decades. Commodity price fluctuations, rapidly shifting geopolitical dynamics, and unprecedented supply disruptions have significantly impacted the energy landscape. Throughout this period, Enbridge—alongside the North American energy industry—has continued to deliver critical energy to homes and businesses around t

he world. "I am proud of the consistency of our performance this quarter, which once again reflects the strength of our diversified, low‑risk business model. Mainline volumes averaged 3.2 million barrels per day and the system has been apportioned all year, highlighting sustained supply and demand from our upstream and downstream partners. In April, we amended presidential permits for a series of pipelines, providing additional operational flexibility and supporting future expansions. We also launched open seasons on the Flanagan South and Southern Access pipelines to support Mainline Optimization Phase 2, advancing an additional 250 kbpd of egress capacity from Canada. "Across our Gas Transmission business, we are advancing high‑quality growth projects, including a 25 Bcf expansion to the Tres Palacios storage facility to offer supply optionality to customers and serve demand needs from  Normalized numeric aliases: 3.2 million = 320만 달러.
